title: News piece outline (arabic-authoring-suite)
description: Schema for a short Arabic news piece (news register, 200-800 words). The smallest of the four content types but with the strictest fact discipline: explicit 5W+H coverage required as a top-level object, not inferred from prose. Inverted-pyramid structure enforced by section ordering.

headline_ar.description: Active-voice news headline. The drafter's news-register prompt explicitly forbids 'In a development that...' opener calques.

lede_ar.description: The lede paragraph (~25-40 Arabic words) that must answer at minimum 4 of the 5Ws.

byline_ar.description: Optional byline. Wire-service pieces typically omit this.

date_iso.description: ISO date. The Arabic-script date rendering happens at draft time.

five_w_h.description: The four mandatory Ws. 'why' and 'how' are recommended but a news piece can ship without them (some breaking-news pieces don't yet have a confirmed motive or mechanism).

sections.description: Body sections. Section 1 is body proper (nut graf + context); optional section 2 is reaction/quote block. Inverted-pyramid: each later section is less essential than the one before.

quotes.description: Direct quotes from sources. Each quote must have an attribution; unattributed quotes are forbidden by news-register policy.

heading_ar.description: Optional. News pieces often have no internal subheads.

attribution_ar.description: e.g., 'وزير الإعلام'. Attribution is mandatory by schema — no 'sources said' filler.

source_ref.description: Citation key from the fact pack where this quote was sourced.
